subject: Social Studies
sample_standards: Your child can explain why people moved to cities and what happened because of this, both good and bad.
Your child can read and compare climate graphs from different places on Earth.
Your child can compare what is expected of men and women in different societies.
Your child can compare the good and bad effects on the economy and the environment of different ways to make energy.
Your child can describe the world using maps and other geography tools.
Your child can understand and judge information shown in tables, graphs, maps, and texts.
Your child can explain what caused World War I, what happened because of it, and why the United States joined the war.
Your child can look at original and secondhand sources to understand their point of view, background, and possible bias.
canonical_name: Grade 6 Social Studies: Social Studies Skills — Geographic, Economic, and Historical Analysis
coherence_reason: The standards cover multiple distinct topics: geographic skills, economic/environmental analysis, gender roles, historical events, and source analysis.

subject: Social Studies
sample_standards: Your child understands that areas can be grouped together because they share things in common, like weather or buildings.
Your child understands how Gestalt ideas help us see and understand things as whole patterns.
Your child learns about shapes by looking at their parts and how they fit together, and practices dividing shapes to understand fractions.
Your child uses tools to identify, describe, and draw special four-sided shapes and sorts flat shapes based on their features.
Your child uses tools to explore the features of triangles and circles.
Your child can observe and describe shapes by their important features, sort and draw them, and begin learning about fractions by dividing shapes.
Your child understands how to use two number lines that cross to find and name points using pairs of numbers called coordinates.
Your child can draw and identify points, lines, line segments, rays, different types of angles, and lines that are perpendicular, parallel, or intersecting.
canonical_name: Grade 6 Math: Geometry — Properties of Shapes and Coordinate Plane
coherence_reason: The standards mix geometry concepts (shapes, properties, coordinates) with unrelated topics such as grouping areas (geography) and Gestalt psychology, indicating poor topical grouping.
split_topics: Geometry: Properties and Classification of Shapes
Geography: Regions and Grouping by Common Features
